hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning generative model is rewarded to maximize CATS-based pharmacophoric similarity to known estrogen receptor antagonists while penalizing MACCS/MAP4-based structural similarity (alongside QED), then it will produce molecules that are more novel yet maintain or improve docking affinity and drug-likeness versus a QED+docking baseline because pharmacophore-focused optimization preserves key interaction patterns while discouraging scaffold reuse.
